experiences.intentions: The active T is high above the stack with lots of lateral freedom. The surface below is irregular, with a notable central/right structure around columns 4-6 and open space on the far left. My goal is to improve future stack shape without committing badly; since immediate hard drop from here is premature, a positioning move is best. The current T orientation is vertical (rotation 1). Rotating right to rotation 2 creates a flat T-up shape that is generally more flexible for eventual placement onto uneven terrain and can later be shifted/dropped. Candidate moves: left/right only reposition while keeping a less versatile vertical shape; soft/hard drop risks an awkward early lock; no_op wastes a turn. Best action now is to rotate into the flatter orientation.

The stack is uneven but there is no immediate danger at the top, so the main goal is to place the Z to reduce surface roughness and avoid creating holes. At the current x-position, the vertical Z can likely drop into the center-right contour around the existing S/I structure more cleanly than spending a move rotating or shifting first. Left/right or rotation this turn delays descent without a clear better fit yet; hard drop risks committing too early if lateral adjustment might still be useful. Soft drop advances toward placement while preserving flexibility next turn.
